forecast-period.text: A high pressure system will slowly approach South Australia from the west throughout today, then move over the State on Friday and Saturday. The high is expected to move to the north of South Australia during Sunday, with a trough to build over the west of the State in the evening.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning frost patches about the northern agricultural area and Flinders district. Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area and West Coast district, mostly contracting to near southern and western coasts in the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, locally fresh at times.

Morning frost patches about the northern agricultural area and Flinders district. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area and West Coast district, mostly contracting to southern coastal districts in the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. West to southwesterly winds in the south. Southeasterly winds over the pastoral districts, fresh at times in the far north.

Patchy morning fog east of the ranges and frost patches about the northern agricultural area and Flinders district. Showers extending over the southern agricultural area during the morning then becoming isolated during the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Northwest to southwesterly winds in the south. East to southeasterly winds in the north.

text.p: Morning frost patches about the northern agricultural area and Flinders district. Isolated showers about the southern agricultural area. Maximum temperatures near average about southern coastal districts but below average elsewhere. Northwesterly winds in the south but generally light and variable winds in the north.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: generally 5 to 10 mm about the southern agricultural area but falls of 10 to 15 mm are possible about the Mount Lofty Ranges, Kangaroo Island and the Lower South East districts. Isolated falls of 15 to 20 mm are possible about the Mount Lofty Ranges. Less than 2 mm is likely elsewhere although isolated falls of 2 to 5 mm are possible about parts of the northern agricultural area and near the west coast.
